task_completion.description: Verify that the Wazuh manager is configured to ingest the custom DNS log file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open text editor or terminal command modifying ossec.conf to include a <localfile> block with <location>/var/log/custom_dns.log</location>.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a custom decoder named 'custom-dns' to extract 'srcip' and 'domain'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open text editor modifying local_decoder.xml (usually in /var/ossec/etc/decoders/) showing <decoder name="custom-dns"> and extraction regex for 'srcip' and 'domain'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a detection rule with ID 100050 and level 12 for domains exceeding 60 characters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open text editor modifying local_rules.xml (usually in /var/ossec/etc/rules/) showing <rule id="100050" level="12"> and a regex condition checking for a domain length greater than 60 characters.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Wazuh manager configuration was applied and the service was restarted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands such as 'systemctl restart wazuh-manager' or '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart' executing successfully without syntax errors.

task_completion.description: Verify that the configuration is active and the rule successfully triggers on the malicious domain.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the execution of '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-logtest' or viewing of '/var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.log' showing rule 100050 firing for the domain 'very-long-encoded-string-that-looks-like-base64-exfiltration-data.attacker-site.com'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually modified the Wazuh configuration files using standard tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of standard text editors (nano, vim) or valid shell redirection (echo, sed) targeting the actual Wazuh directories (/var/ossec/etc/), rather than creating mock files in a temporary directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent implemented a length-based check rather than hardcoding the specific malicious domain.
integrity.visual_evidence: In local_rules.xml, the rule condition should use a regex pattern for length (e.g., matching 61+ characters) and MUST NOT explicitly contain the string 'very-long-encoded-string-that-looks-like-base64-exfiltration-data.attacker-site.com'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used genuine Wazuh tools to verify the rule.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the authentic wazuh-logtest interface or actual Wazuh alert logs, rather than a script that simply prints 'Rule 100050 triggered'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target log file: /var/log/custom_dns.log. Sample data file: /home/ga/dns_sample_data.log. Required rule ID: 100050. Required decoder name: custom-dns. Malicious domain sample: very-long-encoded-string-that-looks-like-base64-exfiltration-data.attacker-site.com. Benign domain sample: google.com.
